text: “Yɛ yuori la we, yɛnee zɔnne naŋ tagra zɔnne, yɛ maŋ yeli la, ‘Soba na zaa naŋ pɔ ne a Naaŋmen die, pɔraa zaa ba dɔgle o zu, kyɛ soba na zaa naŋ pɔ ne a salema naŋ be a Naaŋmen die poɔ, ka a pɔraa na do o la.’
translated: When we say this, both old and young, we are saying, 'Anyone who fears his God, all his things will be good for him, but anyone who does not have respect for his God, his things will not be good for him.'

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.

translated: He said, 'Whoever confesses God's word, his father will also confess him, but whoever denies me and my teaching which is God's word, his father will also deny him.'

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.

translated: Even though they had given birth to their children or were pregnant, all the women had to do work. Even when their delivery time came, they would still give birth to their children there.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.

translated: And when his children multiplied on the earth and daughters were born to them, and the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were beautiful, and he took a wife from Noah who was in his generation,

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.

translated: And those who had arrested Jesus took Him to Caiaphas, the high priest's house, where the scribes and the elders had gathered.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.

translated: But Pilate wanted to please the crowd so he released Barabbas to them and handed Jesus over to be crucified.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.

translated: And Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked Him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" And Jesus said to him, "You have said it."

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: A Pilato naŋ da wa nyɛ ka o ba taa lɛ zaa o naŋ na e, kyɛ ka dɔɔne da piili ka o kpɛ a noba poɔ, O da de la koɔ̃ pɛge o nuuri a noba niŋesogɔ, a yele, “N ba baŋ yelzaa a dɔɔ ŋa kũũ eŋɛ; yɛ taa yɛ yɛlɛ.”
translated: And when Pilate saw that he was accomplishing nothing, but rather that a riot was starting, he took water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, "I am innocent of this man's blood; see to it yourselves."

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”

translated: And if they had believed and accepted Jesus to enter into their hearts completely, they would have received eternal life.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Ka naaloŋ wa kyataa a leɛ zɔɔrɔ ne o meŋɛ, a naaloŋ na na le la.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”

text: A bombie mine meŋ da le la pikubo poɔ, zie naŋ ba taa tɛne yaga, ka a bombie buli wieouŋ zaa, a tɛne naŋ ba e zuluŋ zuiŋ.
translated: If a child does not listen to his parents, a home that they have built well, when the child grows up, he will destroy it completely.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.

translated: If they do not have good sense, they will eat the forbidden fruit, but if the woman and man obey God's word that he gave them, they will live forever.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.

translated: And Jesus said to him, "My son, your sins are forgiven you, go in peace, you are healed from your disease."

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.

translated: Are not his brothers James, Joseph, Judas and Simon with us? And are not all his sisters here with us?" And they took offense at him.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”

translated: And if his master's sons come to him at the well and he says, "Where are you going?" And he says to them, "To Gyɔɔn Bati's house."

text: A ziiri na zaa Yeezu naŋ da gaa, a tembilii bee a teŋkponni, bee wɛre poore, ba da maŋ taa la a baalba a wa biŋ ba dare poore, a kyɛ sɔre O ka O vɛŋ ka ba seene O kparoo nɔzoɔrɔ yoŋ; ka a baalba na zaa naŋ da seene O da nyɛ ba sammo.
translated: If you had known Jesus when he came, his disciples and his followers, and other people, they could go to his house and he would sit down and he would talk to them and they would see his face clearly; and his house where they would see he had made their friend.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”

translated: And the Pharisees and the Scribes asked Jesus, "Why do your disciples not follow the tradition of the elders but eat food with unclean hands?"

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Yɛ zagre la a Naaŋmen nɔɛ bare kyɛ nyɔge nensaala wuluu taa kpeɛŋaa.”
translated: If a man does not fear his God, he will not respect any human being.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
